draft.title: ai workspace
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
ai workspace

Building your first draft…

facade.hypothesis: The user may prefer gentle, diffused lighting and depth over harsh contrast and flatness
facade.label: Soft shadows
facade.acceptImplies: They want visual comfort and organic subtlety in the workspace — softer transitions, layered depth, reduced eye strain
facade.rejectImplies: They prefer clarity, definition, and direct contrast — bright backgrounds, crisp edges, minimal ambient effects

facade.hypothesis: Users building AI workspaces prefer a voice that feels approachable and uses humor to reduce friction around technical complexity
facade.label: Casual and witty
facade.acceptImplies: Lean into conversational tone, playful language, and light humor throughout the product
facade.rejectImplies: Shift toward more formal, authoritative, and straightforward communication without personality

evidence.hypothesis: The user may prefer gentle, diffused lighting and depth over harsh contrast and flatness
evidence.implication: They want visual comfort and organic subtlety in the workspace — softer transitions, layered depth, reduced eye strain

facade.hypothesis: Users building AI workspaces prefer high-contrast, minimal color palettes that reduce cognitive load and emphasize content over decoration
facade.label: Dark monochrome interface
facade.acceptImplies: Explore desaturated, dark-mode heavy aesthetics; test black/white/grey hierarchies; probe minimal accent colors
facade.rejectImplies: Move toward warmer palettes, higher saturation, or colorful accent systems; test if users want visual energy rather than restraint

facade.hypothesis: Users building AI workspaces prefer vertical navigation (sidebar) paired with card-based content layout over tabbed navigation with list views
facade.label: Sidebar + cards
facade.acceptImplies: Explore more sidebar-native patterns; test card density and spacing; consider collapsible sidebar states
facade.rejectImplies: Test horizontal navigation (top tabs); explore list-based layouts; investigate dense tabular content organization

facade.hypothesis: Users of AI workspaces prefer fluid, eased animations that make interactions feel continuous rather than instantaneous—reducing cognitive friction and creating a sense of flow.
facade.label: Smooth transitions
facade.acceptImplies: Embrace gradual reveals, ease curves, animated state changes. Workspace feels alive and composed. Users may tolerate slightly slower feedback in exchange for polish.
facade.rejectImplies: Favor instant response and snappy, minimal animation. Users value speed and directness over visual smoothness. Workspace should feel reactive, not choreographed.

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimal, clean interfaces vs. rich visual detail and color
Whether user wants tools always visible vs. hidden/revealed on demand
Whether user responds to encouraging, conversational guidance vs. neutral, direct instruction
Whether user seeks calm, distraction-free focus vs. energetic, stimulating environment
Whether user prefers dense information at a glance vs. progressive disclosure and simplicity
Whether user likes instant feedback and snappy responses vs. smooth, deliberate transitions

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers minimal, clean interfaces vs. rich visual detail and color
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants tools always visible vs. hidden/revealed on demand
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user responds to encouraging, conversational guidance vs. neutral, direct instruction
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user seeks calm, distraction-free focus vs. energetic, stimulating environment
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers dense information at a glance vs. progressive disclosure and simplicity
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user likes instant feedback and snappy responses vs. smooth, deliberate transitions
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: Users building an AI workspace prefer distilled key metrics and overview cards over dense tables or detailed charts
facade.axisTargeted: Information density & scannability — whether the workspace prioritizes quick pattern recognition or comprehensive data exploration
facade.label: Dashboard at a glance
facade.acceptImplies: The workspace should use cards, gauges, sparklines, and prominently featured KPIs. Visual hierarchy and white space matter. Narrative summaries beat raw numbers.
facade.rejectImplies: The workspace can handle denser layouts with more data points visible at once. Tables, detailed charts, and numeric precision are valued. Information density is not a friction point.

facade.hypothesis: The user may prefer to reduce digital harshness and favor eye-comfort features, suggesting they want an AI workspace that feels less fatiguing and more human-centered rather than purely utilitarian
facade.label: Blue light filter
facade.acceptImplies: Prioritize warmth, reduced blue wavelengths, and softer color palettes in the interface; the workspace should feel less sterile and more forgiving to extended use
facade.rejectImplies: The user may be comfortable with bright, high-contrast digital interfaces and doesn't prioritize physical comfort signals in their workspace aesthetic

draft.title: AI Workspace Hub
draft.summary: A focused workspace interface for managing AI tasks, projects, and collaborative workflows. Clean, minimal design with warm neutrals and coral accents.
